campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the business license tax rates that companies pay to operate in the city. Shannon Dragman from the finance department presented the proposed changes to these tax amounts, and council members discussed and voted on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rates being increased or adjusted
Annual tax rate update process
Finance department recommendations on new rates
Council public deliberation and voting on the changes

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to discuss a proposed new rule or regulation. After hearing public input, they moved forward with the ordinance by skipping the usual first reading process and planning to vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather community feedback
New ordinance fast-tracked for approval
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from their previous session and review the city's finances for February. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign several construction contracts and agreements, including work with QLM, Inc., and approved plans for town improvements.
keyTopics: Approval of February 2026 financial and investment reports
Authorization of construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Town improvement projects moving forward
Review of previous meeting minutes from April 7

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve routine meeting minutes and discussed several projects including replacing an elevator at a terminal facility, getting bids for cleaning up homeless encampments, and updating a digital advertising agreement with Clear Channel. The council also approved city funding and donations for a memorial event honoring fallen firefighters.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure repair/upgrade work
Encampment Cleanup Services - new contracts to address homelessness on city streets
Clear Channel Digital Signage Agreement - update to large format advertising on city property
Light the Night for Fallen Firefighters - approved special event with city funding and community donations

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from community members during a public comment period. The main focus was reviewing and deciding on funding for neighborhood grant requests for the upcoming 2026-27 fiscal year, along with updates on how often neighborhoods are meeting with the city.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for community input
Neighborhood meeting attendance and engagement
Approval of previous meeting minutes

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for open positions on various city boards and commissions. Residents were given opportunities to participate both in person and online during the public comment period before the meeting concluded.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online options)
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss purchasing a property at 10480 Finch Avenue. The city is negotiating with the local school district over the price and payment terms for this land. The council also reviewed a program called Silicon Valley Hopper and discussed plans for getting grant funding.
keyTopics: Land purchase negotiation with Cupertino Union School District for 10480 Finch Avenue
Price and payment terms being discussed for the property deal
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. They also approved a new employment agreement with the city's firefighters union covering 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted around Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026 events
New firefighters union contract approved for 2026-2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before discussion

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a meeting to discuss the city's spending plans for the next several years. They reviewed the budget for the upcoming fiscal year, approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, and made updates to how the City Council runs its meetings.
keyTopics: Approved 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
Reviewed next year's operating budget for fiscal year 2026-27
Adopted master plan for Innovation District parks and trails
Updated City Council meeting procedures and protocols

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting on April 29, 2026, to consider a proposal to split up a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine separate residential lots. The developer wants to build nine single-family homes and seven smaller accessory dwelling units on the property, along with one private street to connect them.
keyTopics: Subdivision of 1.02-acre vacant lot at 4103 Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots
Proposal to build 9 single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Election of Council Chair and Vice Chair
Project exempt from environmental review under Assembly Bill 130
